content.problemStatement:
Chef aims to be the richest person in Chefland by his new restaurant franchise. Currently, his assets are worth AAA billion dollars and have no liabilities. He aims to increase his assets by XXX billion dollars per year.

Also, all the richest people in Chefland are not planning to grow and maintain their current worth.

To be the richest person in Chefland, he needs to be worth at least BBB billion dollars. How many years will it take Chef to reach his goal if his value increases by XXX billion dollars each year?
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers A, B and X.
A is Chef's current worth (in billions), B is the target worth (B > A), and X is the yearly increase (X divides B‑A).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer – the number of years required for Chef's worth to reach at least B.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: Difference = 200‑100 = 100, X = 10, years = 100/10 = 10.
Test case 2: Difference = 199‑111 = 88, X = 11, years = 88/11 = 8.
Test case 3: Difference = 200‑190 = 10, X = 10, years = 10/10 = 1.

solution.approach:
For each test case compute the difference D = B - A. Because X is guaranteed to divide D, the answer is simply D / X. This is O(1) per test case.
Algorithm:
1. Read T.
2. Loop T times:
   a. Read A, B, X.
   b. Compute years = (B - A) / X.
   c. Print years.
The algorithm uses only constant extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time computation per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        A = int(next(it)); B = int(next(it)); X = int(next(it))
        out.append(str((B - A) // X))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
